step_by_step_thinking:
The key features mentioned in the case description are well-defined bald patches, small broken hairs around the affected areas, and no scarring. These findings suggest a pattern typical of alopecia areata, where there is sudden loss of hair forming round or oval bare patches. Therefore, the correct diagnosis would be:
\boxed{B} Based on the clinical presentation of well-defined bald patches with small broken hairs but no scarring, along with the absence of signs typically associated with other types of alopecia like Androgenetic alopecia (patterned hair loss), Anagen effluvium (due to chemotherapy or toxins), or Telogen effluvium (generalized shedding due to stressors), the differential points strongly towards Alopecia areata.
